{{Infobox character
| name = Eliadel
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = the King
| affiliations = Traveler
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[Eliadel Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Eliadel, also known as [[Alin]], is the [[Incarnation of Elysia]], a city described as a paradise of gold and jewels. The name Eliadel translates to "The Rising Sun" in an ancient tongue. According to prophecy, a [[Traveler]] from [[Elysia]] would return to lead the city of [[Enosh]] in a struggle against the [[King of Damasca]]. [[Alin]] is identified as the son of [[Torin]] and has been involved in significant historical shifts, including confronting [[King]] [[Zakareth]] and facilitating the opening of the [[Gate of Heaven]]. His actions have been credited with ending sacrifices by removing the Hanging [[Trees]] and addressing the breaking prison of the [[Incarnations]]. While some view him as a good person, others have expressed varying degrees of respect or skepticism regarding his leadership and impact on the territories.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Eliadel]] is a name [[Grandmaster Naraka]] uses for [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Known also as [[Alin]], Eliadel is a [[Traveler]] who arrived following the presumed conflict between [[Simon]] and [[Malachi]]. He maintains a sense of trust in [[Simon]], despite [[Endross]] harboring intentions to present the man's severed head to him. Eliadel bears distinct chain marks upon his arms that possess the ability to shift from black to a bright, flaring white. After being addressed by [[Grandmaster Naraka]] as Eliadel, he ultimately fulfilled his destined role in prophecy by confronting [[King]] [[Zakareth]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is referred to as [[Eliadel]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eliadel]] trusts [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eliadel]] arrived after [[Simon]] and [[Malachi]]'s supposed fight.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eliadel]] has black chain marks on his arms.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eliadel]]'s arm marks can turn from black to a bright, flaring white.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Endross]] intends to bring [[Eliadel]] [[Simon]]'s head.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eliadel]] is a [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Naraka]] addresses [[Alin]] as [[Eliadel]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Eliadel]] has fulfilled his role in prophecy by confronting [[King]] [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Eliadel is seen in the company of [[Grandmaster Naraka]]. Known by several names including [[Alin]], son of [[Torin]], as well as [[Elysia]] or the [[King]], this individual possesses distinctive silver eyes. During their time in the city, Eliadel killed the reigning king. Despite this act, they are described as a good man at heart.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eliadel]] is with [[Grandmaster Naraka]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eliadel]] is also known as [[Alin]], son of [[Torin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eliadel]] is also referred to as [[Elysia]] or the [[King]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eliadel]] has silver eyes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eliadel]] killed the king of the city.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eliadel]] is described as a good man at heart.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“We once only had the Nine Territories, Eliadel,” Naraka went on.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The rest of the Travelers were hurrying after the Grandmaster, trotting away from the black-eyed swordsman, as Alin followed them. ... “Eliadel! Move, now!”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“I would say that Eliadel has fulfilled his role in prophecy,” Lirial said carefully. “He has, at last, confronted King Zakareth, the Evening Star.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“Eliadel is a good man at heart,” a man’s voice said loudly.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
